about.tagline: Designing Healthier Communities Through Evidence-Based Research
about.description: The Design Research for Active Living (DrAL) Lab at Texas A&M University investigates how the built environment shapes physical activity, health, and well-being across diverse communities. Founded by Dr. Chanam Lee & Dr. Xuemei Zhu, our interdisciplinary team bridges landscape architecture, urban planning, public health, and data science.
about.mission: Our mission is to generate rigorous, translatable evidence that informs policies and designs promoting active living — with a particular focus on health equity, aging populations, and underserved communities.

pillars.title: Built Environment & Active Living
pillars.text: How neighborhood design, street networks, parks, and land use shape physical activity across diverse populations

pillars.title: Health Equity & Vulnerable Populations
pillars.text: Addressing disparities in active living opportunities for older adults, low-income communities, and underserved neighborhoods

pillars.title: Evidence-Based Design
pillars.text: Translating empirical research into actionable design guidelines, planning policy, and community interventions

pillars.title: Community-Engaged Research
pillars.text: Partnering with local governments, planners, and residents to co-produce research that addresses real-world challenges

pillars.title: Active Aging & Mobility
pillars.text: Designing age-friendly environments that support independent mobility, reduce fall risks, and promote physical and social engagement among older adults

quotes.text: We want to ensure we are putting the park into the area without displacing people or having it be unequally available.
We are hoping this new longitudinal, case-comparison study will allow us to better understand what impact Mueller has on its residents' physical activity and health.
Healthy aging is the development and maintenance of optimal physical, mental and social well-being.
We are not merely designing buildings; we are reimagining the future of health through architecture.
quotes.source: Texas A&M University College of Architecture

quotes.text: Summer isn't just a break from school — it's a pivotal time that can shape youth health habits.

footer.line1: DrAL Lab · Design Research for Active Living
